methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $100M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Quantum Computing (found 1 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Quantum Computing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Quantum Machines is a Series B-stage quantum computing company that has raised $100M+ to date. The quantum computing sector comp universe includes 3 public companies (IONQ, RGTI, QBTS) and 8 private firms (including IonQ, Quantinuum, Rigetti Computing, D-Wave Quantum, and Q-CTRL), though peers at identical stage and valuation tier remain sparse.

The stage-progression method is the primary applicable valuation approach here. Using total raised ($100M) as a base proxy and applying a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical for Series B progression yields an implied valuation of $190M. This reflects the expected increase in company value between Series B and later funding rounds, conditional on execution risk remaining within historical norms for the stage.

The peer-median approach was not applicable given insufficient same-stage and adjacent-stage comparables within quantum computing (only 1 same-stage peer identified; analysis requires ≥3 datapoints for statistical reliability). A triangulation based solely on stage progression supports a valuation range of $162M–$218M, with a central estimate of $190M. Key uncertainties include the actual dilution embedded in the $100M+ total raised figure, technology differentiation relative to the fragmented public comp set, and near-term capital intensity requirements typical for hardware-intensive quantum ventures.
